---
title: "Variables over Time"
toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

{{< include _setup.qmd >}}

```{r}
pop_affected <- read_csv(here('data/processed/pop_affected.csv'))
wfbz <- wfbz %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(usfs_region = wfbz_region)
```

```{r}
# function to format all numeric columns with commas and handle decimals appropriately,
# and to also include character variables
texify <- function(var_name, var_value) {
  # Convert var_name to valid LaTeX command name (no underscores)
  latex_name <- gsub("_", "", var_name)

  # handle numeric values
  if (is.numeric(var_value) && length(var_value) == 1 && !is.na(var_value)) {
    # check if the number is an integer (no decimal part)
    is_integer_like <- (var_value %% 1 == 0)

    if (is_integer_like) {
      # for integers, just add commas without decimal places
      formatted_value <- format(
        var_value,
        big.mark = ",",
        scientific = FALSE,
        nsmall = 0
      )
    } else {
      # for decimals, round to two decimal places
      rounded_value <- round(var_value, 2)
      formatted_value <- format(
        rounded_value,
        big.mark = ",",
        scientific = FALSE,
        nsmall = 2
      )
    }

    paste0("\\newcommand{\\", latex_name, "}{", trimws(formatted_value), "}")
  } else if (
    is.character(var_value) && length(var_value) == 1 && !is.na(var_value)
  ) {
    # handle character values
    # Escape % for LaTeX
    escaped_value <- gsub("%", "\\\\%", var_value)
    # Use brackets [] for CIs instead of parentheses ()
    escaped_value <- gsub("\\(([^)]+)\\)", "[\\1]", escaped_value)
    paste0("\\newcommand{\\", latex_name, "}{", escaped_value, "}")
  } else {
    NULL
  }
}

more_than <- function(x, by) {
  floor(x / by) * by
}

# round UP to the nearest `by` -- for "nearly X" phrasing (true value sits
# just below the stated round number)
nearly <- function(x, by) {
  ceiling(x / by) * by
}
```

```{r}
### Abstract
abstract <- list()
abstract[['nWFBZ']] = nrow(wfbz) # nationwide spatial dataset of N WFBZ disasters
abstract[['alaskaMeanPopExposed']] <- pop_affected %>%
  filter(region == 'Alaska') %>%
  summarize(exposed_10km = mean(exposed_10km)) %>%
  pull(exposed_10km) %>%
  scales::comma() # estimated number of people affected by WFBZ disasters annually varied widely across the US, ranging from 3,300 people exposed in Alaska
abstract[['californiaMeanPopExposedText']] <- pop_affected %>%
  filter(region == 'California') %>%
  summarize(exposed_10km = mean(exposed_10km)) %>%
  pull(exposed_10km) %>%
  more_than(by = 1e6) %>%
  xfun::numbers_to_words()
abstract[['hawaiiPctOfDisasters']] <- mean(wfbz$wildfire_states == 'HI') %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # Hawaii: 0.3\% of all WFBZ disasters
abstract[['hawaiiPctOfFatalities']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(tot_fatal = sum(wildfire_max_civil_fatalities, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(wildfire_states == 'HI') %>%
  summarize(
    pct = sum(wildfire_max_civil_fatalities / tot_fatal, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # Hawaii accounted for 18.6\% of fatalities
abstract[['californiaPctFEMA']] <- wfbz %>%
  filter(grepl('CA', wildfire_states)) %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # california: 26.2\% of its fires received declarations
abstract[['hawaiiPctFEMA']] <- wfbz %>%
  filter(grepl('HI', wildfire_states)) %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # Hawaii: 83\% of its fires received declarations
abstract[['washingtonPctFEMA']] <- wfbz %>%
  filter(grepl('WA', wildfire_states)) %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # Washington: 49\% of its fires received declarations
abstract[['nationalMeanPopExposed']] <- pop_affected %>%
  summarize(exposed_10km = mean(exposed_10km)) %>%
  pull(exposed_10km) %>%
  scales::comma() # an estimated 551,909 people were exposed annually at the national level
abstract[['totalPopExposed']] <- pop_affected %>%
  summarize(exposed_10km = sum(exposed_10km, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(exposed_10km) %>%
  scales::comma() # exact count behind: "Nearly [totalPopExposedText] US residents were exposed to WFBZ disasters from 2000-2025"
abstract[['totalPopExposedText']] <- pop_affected %>%
  summarize(exposed_10km = sum(exposed_10km, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(exposed_10km) %>%
  nearly(by = 5e6) %>%
  xfun::numbers_to_words() # "Nearly <X> US residents were exposed to WFBZ disasters from 2000-2025" (rounded up to nearest 5M for the "nearly" phrasing)

### results
results <- list()

results[['alaskaMeanFireSize']] <- wfbz %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Alaska') %>%
  summarize(mean_size = mean(wildfire_area, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(mean_size) # Fires in Alaska were the largest on average (mean: 144.3 km\textsuperscript{2}


results[['nRegionsLargeFireSize']] <- wfbz %>%
  filter(usfs_region != 'Alaska') %>%
  group_by(usfs_region) %>%
  summarize(mean_size = mean(wildfire_area, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(mean_size > 90) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_size)) %>%
  pull(usfs_region) %>%
  length() %>%
  xfun::numbers_to_words() # five regions that had mean fire sizes exceeding 90km2 (should be a string: "Intermountain, Pacific Northwest, Northern Rockies, California, and Southwestern")

results[['regionsLargeFireSize']] <- wfbz %>%
  group_by(usfs_region) %>%
  summarize(mean_size = mean(wildfire_area, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(mean_size > 90) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_size)) %>%
  pull(usfs_region) %>%
  knitr::combine_words() # five regions that had mean fire sizes exceeding 90km2 (should be a string: "Intermountain, Pacific Northwest, Northern Rockies, California, and Southwestern")

results[['nationalMeanPopExposed']] <- pop_affected %>%
  summarize(exposed_10km = mean(exposed_10km)) %>%
  pull(exposed_10km) %>%
  scales::comma() # annually 551,909 people were exposed to WFBZ disasters nationwide

abstract[['californiaMeanPopExposedText']] <- pop_affected %>%
  filter(region == 'California') %>%
  summarize(exposed_10km = mean(exposed_10km)) %>%
  pull(exposed_10km) %>%
  scales::comma()

results[['wuiPctRange']] <- wfbz %>%
  group_by(usfs_region) %>%
  summarize(
    wui_pct = sum(
      str_detect(wildfire_wui, '(intermix|interface)'),
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) /
      n()
  ) %>%
  summarize(
    rng = paste0(
      scales::percent(min(wui_pct), accuracy = .1),
      ' to ',
      scales::percent(max(wui_pct), accuracy = .1)
    )
  ) %>%
  pull(rng) # proportion of fires occurring in the WUI ranged from 40.6\% to 83.3\% across regions

results[['nationalWuiPct']] <- wfbz %>%
  summarize(
    wui_pct = sum(
      str_detect(wildfire_wui, '(intermix|interface)'),
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) /
      n()
  ) %>%
  pull(wui_pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # national mean proportion of fires in the wui

results[['hawaiiFatalityRate']] <- wfbz_crit %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Hawaii') %>%
  summarize(rate = sum(`Fatalities per 100K`, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(rate) # Fatality rates were highest in Hawaii (6.7 per 100,000 people in that region)

results[['californiaFatalityRate']] <- wfbz_crit %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'California') %>%
  summarize(rate = sum(`Fatalities per 100K`, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(rate) # fatality rates in California (0.58 per 100,000 people in that region)

results[['hawaiiRegionDecRate']] <- wfbz %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Hawaii') %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1)
results[['pnwDecRate']] <- wfbz %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Pacific Northwest') %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1)
results[['southDecRate']] <- wfbz %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Southern') %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1)
results[['southNumDisasters']] <- sum(wfbz$usfs_region == 'Southern')
results[['southPctOfDisasters']] <- (sum(wfbz$usfs_region == 'Southern') /
  length(wfbz$usfs_region)) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = 1) # Hawaii had the highest declaration rate (83.3\%), followed by the Pacific Northwest (48.4\%), while the South had the lowest (6.2\%) despite containing the largest share of WFBZ disasters nationally (n = 3,014, 51\% of total WFBZ disasters).

results[[
  'disproportionateStates'
]] <- "California, Florida, Mississippi, Oklahoma, and South Carolina" # Several states accounted for a disproportionate share of events, including California, Florida, Mississippi, Oklahoma, and South Carolina (this should be the string of these states)

results[['californiaAnnualBurnedArea']] <- wfbz_crit %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'California') %>%
  summarize(mean_area = mean(`Burned Area`, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(mean_area) # California consistently had the largest total burned area (annual avg for CA)

results[['southAnnualEvents']] <- wfbz_crit %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Southern') %>%
  summarize(mean_n = mean(Events, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(mean_n) # South consistently had the largest number of annual WFBZ disasters (annual avg for the south)

results[['californiaPctOfFatalities']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(tot = sum(wildfire_max_civil_fatalities, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(grepl('CA', wildfire_states)) %>%
  summarize(
    pct = sum(wildfire_max_civil_fatalities / tot, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # California accounted for 43.2\% of fatalities

results[['californiaPctOfStructures']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(tot = sum(wildfire_struct_destroyed, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(grepl('CA', wildfire_states)) %>%
  summarize(
    pct = sum(wildfire_struct_destroyed / tot, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # California accounted for 69.8\% of structures destroyed nationwide

results[['californiaPctOfFEMA']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(tot = sum(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(grepl('CA', wildfire_states)) %>%
  summarize(pct = sum(wildfire_fema_dec / tot, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # California accounted for 23.4\% of FEMA declarations

results[['southPctOfFatalities']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(tot = sum(wildfire_max_civil_fatalities, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Southern') %>%
  summarize(
    pct = sum(wildfire_max_civil_fatalities / tot, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # The south accounted for 15.0\% of fatalities

results[['southPctOfStructures']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(tot = sum(wildfire_struct_destroyed, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Southern') %>%
  summarize(
    pct = sum(wildfire_struct_destroyed / tot, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # The south accounted for 13.5\% of structures destroyed nationwide

results[['southPctOfFEMA']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(tot = sum(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(usfs_region == 'Southern') %>%
  summarize(pct = sum(wildfire_fema_dec / tot, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # The South accounted for 20.5\% of FEMA declarations

results[['nationalFemaDecRate']] <- wfbz %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(wildfire_fema_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) # Nationally, 15.5\% of WFBZ disasters received a FEMA FMAG declaration
# Manuscript sentence "[California's] share of civilian fatalities (43.5\%) and
# structures destroyed (69.8\%), despite having a lower share of FMAG
# declarations (23.4\%)" is already covered by results$californiaPctOfFatalities
# / californiaPctOfStructures / californiaPctOfFEMA above.

results[[
  'fiveDeadliestFires'
]] <- "the Yarnell Hill Fire (AZ, 2013), Tubbs Fire (CA, 2017), Camp Fire (CA, 2018), North Complex Fire (CA, 2020), and Lahaina Fire (HI, 2023)" # Generally, structural losses and fatalities were heavily concentrated in five events: the Yarnell Hill Fire (AZ, 2013), Tubbs Fire (CA, 2017), Camp Fire (CA, 2018), North Complex Fire (CA, 2020), and Lahaina Fire (HI, 2023) -- should be a string of these 5 fires and their state/date

# (already computed in abstract as hawaiiPctOfFatalities)
# hawaii accounted for 18.6\% of fatalities

results[['hawaiiNumDisasters']] <- sum(grepl('HI', wfbz$wildfire_states)) # fema declarations: Hawaii -- 83\% of 18 disasters
results[['pnwNumDisasters']] <- sum(wfbz$usfs_region == 'Pacific Northwest') # fema declarations: PNW 49\% of 341 disasters
results[['californiaNumDisasters']] <- sum(grepl('CA', wfbz$wildfire_states)) # fema declarations: 26.2\% of California's 816 WFBZ disasters received declarations

results[['femaDecPctInWUI']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(
    in_wui = if_else(
      is.na(wildfire_wui),
      FALSE,
      str_detect(wildfire_wui, '(intermix|interface)')
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(wildfire_fema_dec) %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(in_wui)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = .1) #  82.5\% of WFBZ disasters that received a FEMA declaration occurred in the WUI

# PLACEHOLDER: comment text is ambiguous -- says "received a fema declaration
# occurred outside the wui", but a literal reading contradicts the prior
# 82.5\% in-WUI value. Likely intended contrast: \% of NON-FEMA-declared
# fires in the WUI (so the two values describe FEMA vs. non-FEMA in-WUI rates).
# Computing that interpretation; user should verify.
results[['nonFemaDecPctInWUI']] <- wfbz %>%
  mutate(
    in_wui = if_else(
      is.na(wildfire_wui),
      FALSE,
      str_detect(wildfire_wui, '(intermix|interface)')
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!wildfire_fema_dec) %>%
  summarize(pct = mean(in_wui)) %>%
  pull(pct) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = 1) # compared to 58\% of wfbz disasters that received a fema declaration occurred outside the wui

### discussion
discussion <- list()

discussion[['caSouthPctExposed']] <- pop_affected %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarize(total_exposed = sum(exposed_10km)) %>%
  mutate(pct = total_exposed / sum(total_exposed)) %>%
  filter(region %in% c('California', 'Southern')) %>%
  summarize(combined = .05 * ceiling(sum(pct) / .05)) %>%
  pull(combined) %>%
  scales::percent(accuracy = 1) # California and the South together accounted for nearly 80\% total people exposed annually nationwide
```


```{r}
f <- here('tex/vals.tex')
write_lines('% VALUES FOR MANUSCRIPT %', f)
write_lines('% ABSTRACT %', f, append = TRUE)
write_lines(
  map(names(abstract), \(.x) texify(.x, abstract[[.x]])),
  f,
  append = TRUE
)
write_lines('% RESULTS %', f, append = TRUE)
write_lines(
  map(names(results), \(.x) texify(.x, results[[.x]])),
  f,
  append = TRUE
)
write_lines('% DISCUSSION %', f, append = TRUE)
write_lines(
  map(names(discussion), \(.x) texify(.x, discussion[[.x]])),
  f,
  append = TRUE
)
```
